original_hypothesis.title: Deployment script deploys with empty allowedTokens list: protocol usability and withdrawal/claim liveness may depend on later allowToken calls
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The provided Foundry script deploys PrelaunchPoints with an empty allowedTokens array. Only WETH is auto-allowed in the constructor. If production deployment repeats this pattern unintentionally, all intended LRT deposits will revert (TokenNotAllowed), causing protocol launch failure or user lock-in to ETH/WETH only.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a realistic configuration gap: scripts are often used in production. While not an on-chain exploit by an attacker, misconfiguration can materially disrupt protocol operations and user expectations.
deployment_assumptions: Production uses PrelaunchPoints.s.sol as-is or similarly passes an empty allowedTokens array
Owner does not call allowToken for intended LRTs before users attempt to lock them
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: PrelaunchPoints.s.sol defines address[] public allowedTokens; never populated before new PrelaunchPoints(EXCHANGE_PROXY, WETH, allowedTokens).

proof_of_signal.pattern: address[] public allowedTokens; // never set; passed into constructor

preconditions: Deployment uses script/config that passes empty allowedTokens
Users attempt to lock LRT tokens before owner allowlists them

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: LRT deposits revert unexpectedly; could break points program and user flows
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No constructor requirement that allowedTokens length > 0; no events confirming initial allowlist set

analysis_questions: Is there a separate production deployment process that populates allowedTokens?
Is allowToken intended to be called immediately post-deploy?
Do docs/UI assume specific LRTs are depositable from block 1?
validation_requirements: Review actual deployment pipeline and parameters for mainnet
Check if allowToken transactions are planned/automated before launch
Add deployment-time checks or post-deploy verification scripts

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not attacker-driven: users attempting to lock intended LRTs will revert, potentially causing loss of user trust and broken program timelines until owner corrects via allowToken.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: No direct loss, but major operational disruption; users may miss points accrual window
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: High (owner can allowToken), but requires timely admin action

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No deployment verification tests ensuring allowlist contains expected tokens

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediate: TokenNotAllowed reverts for all non-WETH tokens

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
